clinical_history: ECG (with noisy baseline) from a critically-ill elderly man shows all of the following except:
diagnosis: e) The first four beats show a complete left bundle branch block (LBBB) pattern (not accelerated idioventricular rhythm) which abruptly disappears at a nearly identical heart rate.  An acute inferior myocardial infarction (MI) pattern is apparent in both sets of beats, with reciprocal ST depressions in I, aVL and V2-V3.  LBBB can mask the diagnosis of acute or prior MI.  In this somewhat unusual case, the acute MI is readily confirmable in the normally conducted beats.
